claims.evidence_text: Wheat is considered ready to harvest when grain moisture content is below 15% or spikelets are yellowing.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat is ready to harvest when grain moisture content is below 15% (or spikelets yellowing); grain typically standardized to 12% moisture for yield reporting.

claims.evidence_text: Physiological maturity is the point at which grain reaches hard dough stage, i.e. when spikes completely lose their green color; it typically occurs a few days before harvestable maturity.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat physiological maturity is reached at hard dough stage when spikes completely lose green color, a few days before harvestable maturity.

claims.evidence_text: Harvest index = Dry weight of grain (kg/ha) / Total aboveground biomass (kg/ha)
claims.claim_summary: Wheat harvest index is defined as grain dry weight divided by total aboveground biomass (both kg/ha).
